observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in compliance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We strongly recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) To supervise and urge the assigned personnel to comply with
the above-mentioned provisions of the COA’s Manual on
Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles;
b) There shall be an established clear guidelines and protocols on
the proper usage of government vehicles, including the detailed
procedures on the requisition and issuance of fuel to the
authorized officials. The regular issuance of driver’s trip
tickets shall be religiously complied with and Monthly Report
of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted for
audit; and
c) Designate an employee who will be assigned to prepare,
monitor and submit the required Monthly Fuel Consumption
Report.

2.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a. Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, in compliance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume I;
b. General Services Officer shall maintain and update his
property cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Municipal Accountant shall maintain and update his property
ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant shall reconcile their property cards and property
ledger cards, respectively; and
e. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January
31, of each year.

3.4 We recommended to call the attention of all concerned employees
with unsettled Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances to settle the same
within the reglementary period of time.

4.8 We recommend that the Bids and Award Committee prepares the
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) with the approval by the Head of the
Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester and
the Annual Procurement Plan to GPPB on or before end of January of the
budget year.

5.7 Therefore, we highly recommend that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) to instruct the:
a) Municipal Accountant should also submit the Report of
Disbursement, at the lower portion of the Report of
Disbursement (RD) is a summary of the cash transactions for
the month. It contains the beginning balance, total cash
received by the agency, total disbursements and the ending
cash in bank balance; and
b) Be instructed to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation
Statement to properly monitor the Cash in Bank Balance.

6.4 We have recommended the management to review all necessary
documents before submission and see to it that all where in
compliance to various sections stated in the Manual of Procedures for
the procurement of Infrastructure.

a) Notify the public through signboards of their projects at its location and to provide the Commission on Audit the list of the on-going projects/programs/ activities and those that are to be implemented during the year; and b) Adhere to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended the management to furnished the Audit Team Contract documents for infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the contract was perfected, in compliance to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, in order for the audit team to consider necessary review and evaluation of such and informing the management of some defects/deficiencies, if any.

The management shall strictly comply with the Section 100 of the PD 1445: Reports of disbursing officers in a government agency and the Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code: Failure of accountable officers to render accounts – in submission of the regular report promptly in in time frame given.
observations.reason: Some where not yet submitted due to time constraint.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management submit the documents on emergency procured goods and services to the Office of the Auditor and comply with the documentary requirements under Government Procurement Policy Board Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 for future transactions.

Recommendation
 Notify the public through signboards of their projects at its location and to provide
the Commission on Audit the list of the on-going projects/programs/activities and
those that are to be implemented during the year; and
 Adhere to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

Recommendation
We recommended the management to furnished the Audit Team of the
contract documents for infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the
contract was perfected, in compliance to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001
dated February 12, 2009, in order for the audit team to consider necessary review and
evaluation of such and informing the management of some defects/deficiencies, if any.

Recommendation
The Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance to Section 74 of the
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing
Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to
the auditor for review and verification.

Recommendation
The management shall strictly comply with the Section 100 of the PD 1445:
Reports of disbursing officers in a government agency and the Article 218 of the
Revised Penal Code: Failure of accountable officers to render accounts – in
submission of the regular report promptly in in time frame given.

Recommendation
We recommended that the account Property, Plant and Equipment totaling to
P 199,261,159.99 ON ALL FUNDS must be evaluated, thus, submission of the Report
on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in violation to
Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992, Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the
Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines is deemed necessary.

Recommendation
We recommended that the management submit the required documents on
emergency procured goods and services to the Office of the Auditor and comply with
the documentary requirements under Government Procurement Policy Board
Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 for future transactions.

We recommend that the Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment be annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2019 to this Commission, in accordance to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

It is recommended that the management require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to render their accounts and submit the same for audit in accordance to Section 347 of Republic Act (R.A) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 199, Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 7.2.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

It is recommended that the management should submit the supporting documents of the twenty-none (29) paid disbursement vouchers inclusive of the Consultancy Contract showing an urgent and immediate need of such services and include a clear and detailed statements of the consultant’s responsibilities to give assurance that such services are neither duplication of works of incumbent Municipal Officials nor beyond the capability of the municipality to undertake and substantiate whether the purpose for it was achieved.

We recommend that the management should limit the granting of cash advances to the Accountable Officer for payroll and petty cash expenses or for transactions where cash advance is expressly authorized by law. For miscellaneous expenses, the amount shall be sufficient to cover petty expenses for every fifteen days only, pursuant to Section 89 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.1,3.1..1 to 3.1.4,3.2,3.2.1, and 3.2.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof, pursuant to Section 2.1, 3.1, 3.1.1 to 3.1.3, 3.2 and 3.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall personally supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under existing policies; improve recordkeeping and thereby affecting the timeliness of submission of the Commission on Audit (COA) audit reports, contrary to Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, Section 70(2) and 73(1)(2) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Unit, Volume I, Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2002-003 and Sections 44 and 48 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

1. We recommended that the Supply or Property Officer shall maintain the Property Card for each class of equipment/property to record the acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, depreciation, disposal and other information about the equipment/property based on the source documents of the transactions. The basis of recording the issuance of furniture/equipment is either the RIV, PR, IR or RIS. The Equipment/Property Cards shall be reconciled with the Equipment/Property Ledger Cards maintained by the accounting office. Furthermore, it was recommended to conduct annual physical count on the Property, Plant and Equipment and submit to the Office of the Auditor the required Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE).
observations.reason: manageme nt has not yet started to conduct annual physical inventory based on the required documents

observations.recommendation: 2. The Municipal Mayor shall cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies to ensure the timeliness of the verification of the reports by the LGU as well as the Auditor.
observations.reason: manageme nt is still committing a delay in their internal control system.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management require the Inventory Committee to
conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually
taking into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we
recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2019 to this Commission, in accordance to Section
156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992,
Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS), Volume I, and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445 known as
the Government Auditing Code of The Philippines.

It is recommended that the management require the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to render their accounts and submit the same for audit in accordance with
Section 347 of Republic Act (R.A) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local
Government Code of 1991, Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as Government Code of the Philippines and Section 7.2.1 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

It is recommended that the management should submit the supporting
documents of the twenty-none (29) paid disbursement vouchers inclusive of the
Consultancy Contract showing an urgent and immediate need of such services and
include a clear and detailed statements of the consultant’s responsibilities to give
assurance that their scope of work are neither duplication of works of incumbent
Municipal Officials nor beyond the capability of the municipality to undertake and
substantiate whether the purpose for it was hired was achieved. We further recommend
to avoid processing such payments without complete documentation/attachments and
follow the provisions stated in Section 5(i) of the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations (RIRR) of the Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government
Procurement Reform Act.

We recommend that the management should limit the granting of cash advances to the
Accountable Officer for payroll and petty cash expenses or for transactions where cash
advance is expressly authorized by law. For miscellaneous expenses, the amount shall be
sufficient to cover petty expenses for every fifteen days only, pursuant to Section 89 of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines and Sections 3.1,3.1..1 to 3.1.4,3.2,3.2.1, and 3.2.2 of the Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and
purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001,
together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings,
abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of
procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the
technical evaluation thereof, pursuant to Section 2.1, 3.1, 3.1.1 to 3.1.3, 3.2 and 3.2.1 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. ; thus,
defeating the purpose of the timely audit of transactions and inspection of deliveries.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall cause and personally
supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports
within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies; improve recordkeeping
and documentation and effective implementation of internal control system, pursuant
to Section 100 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines, Section 70(2) and 73(1)(2) of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government
Unit, Volume I, Section 2.0 of the Commission on Audit (COA Circular No. 2002-2003
and Sections 44 and 48 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-382 dated
July 3, 1992.

3. We recommend that the officials concerned submit immediately the subject disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports due for submission to avoid suspensions or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: management was able to submit those the requirements.

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommend the procuring entity to submit copy of the contract and PIOs within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: management was able to submit the perfected contracts, purchase order and inspection reports that were initially not submitted to this Office.

observations.recommendation: 5. We require the accountant to adhere strictly to section 4 and 5 of Volume I and section 31 of Volume II of NGAS Manual for LGUs. Likewise, to prepare and submit JEVs, and submit DVs.
observations.managementAction: management has already submitted their JEVs and was attached to its corresponding DVs.

observations.recommendation: 6. The municipality should adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the purchase for meals and snacks shall be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: management has submitted the complete documents for these transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management avail of the services provided by the LBP through an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for payment of salaries to its permanent employees as well as casual and job order employees to promote operational efficiency as well as strengthened internal control on cash and to avoid further the risk of possible loss/misapplication.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Management has not yet started to set-up ATM accounts for payment of salaries of employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the General Services Office should complete and enhance their property records and coordinate with Accounting Office in reducing if not totally eliminating the unreconciled difference between their records. We further recommend that the concerned officials should conduct physical inventory in a soonest possible time and submit to the Office of the Auditor a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE).
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Management has not yet started to conduct physical inventory of their Property, Plant and Equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management should require the Inventory
Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and
Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and
procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2018 to
this Commission.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to
submit immediately all paid disbursement vouchers with original supporting
documents to the Auditor’s Office. Furthermore, the Municipal Mayor shall
cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final
reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame
prescribed under the existing policies.

We recommended that officials concerned submit immediately the
subject disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports due
for submission to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts
and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA
Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution,
minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to
the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in
the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly the
provisions of Sections 4(e) and 5 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I and Section 31 of the same Manual, Volume II. Likewise, direct
her to prepare and submit immediately the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs),
Check Disbursement Journal (CkDJ), Cash Disbursement Journal (CDJ)
and Cash Receipts Journal (CRJ).

The Municipality should adhere to the principle of transparency,
accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.
In addition, purchases for meals and snacks shall be supported with
complete documentation.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992. Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 management were able to submit the required document.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to call the attention of all concerned employees with unsettled Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances to settle the same within the reglementary period of time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 management was able to settle their Notice of Suspensions.

observations.recommendation: The Management should open a separate special trust fund account. The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end should accrue to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Management were able to transfer their unexpended balance of LDRRMF to special trust fund account.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that management avail of the services provided by the LBP through an
Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for payment of salaries to its permanent employees as
well as casual and job order personnel in order to promote operational efficiency as well as
strengthened internal control on cash and to avoid further the risk of possible
loss/misapplication.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance
with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992. Such BRS shall be regularly
submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

We recommend that the General Services Office should complete and enhance
their property records and coordinate with Accounting Office in reducing if not totally
eliminating the unreconciled difference between their records. We further recommend
that the concerned officials should conduct physical inventory in a soonest possible
time and submit to the Office of the Auditor a Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE).

Recommendations:
 The Management should open a separate special trust fund account.
 The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end should accrue to a special trust fund solely
for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the
LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.

We recommended to call the attention of all concerned employees with unsettled
Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances to settle the same within the reglementary period
of time.

recommended the
management to
conduct annual
physical inventory
of all its PPE; 
and for Municipal
Accountant and
GSO-Designate to
maintain a
complete and
updated Subsidiary
Ledgers and
Property cards,
respectively, and
have their records
reconcile with
each other, in

the accounting

property prescribe

Moreover, they
should be
submitting their

We recommended that the Supply or Property Officer shall maintain the Property Card for each class of equipment/property to record the acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, depreciation, disposal and other information about the equipment/property based on the source documents of the transactions. The basis of recording the issuance of furniture/equipment is either the RIV, PR, IR or RIS. The Equipment/Property Cards shall be reconciled with the Equipment/Property Ledger Cards maintained by the accounting office.
Furthermore, it was recommended to conduct annual physical count on the Property, Plant and Equipment and submit to the Office of the Auditor the required Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE).

We recommend that the payment of procured items should be made directly to the supplier/dealer and stop the practice of paying procured supplies and materials thru reimbursement and the appropriate procurement procedure shall be strictly adhered to. Furthermore, all the required documents shall be complied and complete to ensure that the approval of these disbursement vouchers should first be sought before payments are made.

We recommended that the government officials should claim for traveling expenses only within the prescribed limits set forth under Section 6 of E.O. No. 298 and must be supported with complete documentation.

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

The Municipal Mayor shall cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies to ensure the timeliness of reports by the LGU as well as the Auditor.
